rulings.Case Title: ALI AHMAD, MD VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 7, 2025, line 1, PETITIONER ALI AHMAD, MD'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate Continued to January 21, 2025, on the court's motion. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: JESUS CHAVEZ ET AL VS. MARIE GRACE JENNIFER PASILABAN WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For An Order For Imposition Of Issue, Evidentiary, And/Or Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Jesus Chavez And Raul Garcia For Violation Of Court Orderq
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 7, 2025, line 2, DEFENDANT MARIE GRACE PASILABAN WONG, TRUSTEE OF THE PHILIP MACDONALD FAMILY TRUST'S Motion For An Order For Imposition Of Issue, Evidentiary, And/Or Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiffs Jesus Chavez And Raul Garcia For Violation Of Court Order The motion is granted as to request numbers 1, 3, 4, 10, 15, 19, 21, 23, 24, 27. 28, 30, 31, 3. Requested information is reasonably calculated to lead to admissible evidence regarding the willfulness of Defendant's conduct. Defendant has not established these are objectionable. Defendant's argument that a piece of new legislation that it concedes does not apply to this action, is the final word on the discoverability of these documents is unpersuasive. The motion is denied as to request numbers 2, 6, 12, 32, and 33. Defendant has made a diligent inquiry and located no documents in response. Accordingly, there is no further response to for the Court to order. The Motion is Denied as to request number 5, 7, 8, 9, 11, 13, 14, 20, 22, 25, 26, 29, 34. Requests are overbroad, vague, or seek unduly burdensome discovery. "Trial judges must carefully weigh the cost, time, expense and disruption of normal business resulting from an order compelling the discovery against the probative value of the material which might be disclosed if the discovery is ordered. (Calcor Space Facility, Inc. v. Superior Ct., 53 Cal. App. 4th 216, 223.) Motion is Denied to requests numbers 16, 17, and 18. Requests are duplicative of previous requests. Request for Sanctions is Denied. "[T]he court shall impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel further response to a demand unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." (CCP Section 2031.310(h).) The Court finds both sides acted with substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO S. ARIAS VS. VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 7, 2025, line 6, PLAINTIFF EDUARDO ARIAS' AN INDIVIDUAL Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One Off calendar for moving and opposing party's failure to provide courtesy copies in compliance with local rule 2.7B. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address = (302/BZ)
